_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: GET /project/{project_id}/docs/{doc_id}/pdf raises unhandled PermissionError -> HTTP 500 when on-disk PDF cache dir is not writable

steps.description: Backend: in dashboard/routers/docs.py::docs_pdf, wrap the cache_dir.mkdir(...) + cache_file.write_bytes(...) + svc.update_doc(...) block at lines 320-324 in try/except Exception, mirroring the existing guard at lines 256-266 in docs_pdf_view (same `# noqa: BLE001 — read-only fs, permission error, etc.` comment, same `import logging` inside except, same `logging.getLogger(__name__).warning('Failed to write pdf_path cache for doc %s/%s', project_id, doc_id)`). The Response return after the block stays exactly as-is — the PDF bytes are returned unconditionally. Do NOT edit docs_pdf_view, do NOT refactor into a shared helper, do NOT touch test files. Targeted verification: `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py -v --no-cov` — expect 1 XPASS(strict) on the docs-pdf case (the strict-xfail flip is the RED->GREEN signal; S03 removes the EXPECTED_5XX entry).

Per-agent review of S01: the try/except guard is a verbatim mirror of docs_pdf_view's pattern (same comment, same logger call, same warning message); the Response return on the happy path is unchanged (NOT moved inside try); files_changed contains only dashboard/routers/docs.py; docs_pdf_view is byte-identical to main; no refactor into a shared helper; tdd_red_evidence captures the XPASS(strict) flip on the route-sweep case.

Tests: (a) add tests/dashboard/test_docs_pdf_cache_failure.py::test_docs_pdf_returns_200_when_cache_dir_not_writable — patches Path.mkdir to raise PermissionError for the docs '.generated' cache dir, patches dashboard.routers.docs.render_pdf_chromium to return deterministic bytes, drives the docs_pdf route via the dashboard `client` fixture, asserts SEMANTIC correctness (status==200, content-type=='application/pdf', body.startswith(b'%PDF'), Content-Disposition has 'attachment', WARNING log records the exact 'Failed to write pdf_path cache for doc' message, AND db_session.refresh(doc); doc.pdf_path is None); (b) remove the EXPECTED_5XX entry for '/project/{project_id}/docs/{doc_id}/pdf' from tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py:142 — preserve the surrounding `EXPECTED_5XX: dict[str, str] = {}` declaration and the explanatory comment. Targeted verification only: `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_docs_pdf_cache_failure.py tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py -v --no-cov` — do NOT call make test-unit or make test-integration. Test file MUST live under tests/dashboard/ (the `client` fixture is registered only there per I-00067).

Per-agent review of S03: every assertion in the new test is SEMANTIC (specific status code, specific content-type, PDF magic bytes prefix, specific warning substring, doc.pdf_path is None) — not shape-only; the test file lives under tests/dashboard/; the EXPECTED_5XX entry removal is in the same commit as the new test; no runtime source-revert / git stash in the step; no full-suite run; files_changed limited to the two test files (no dashboard/routers/docs.py touch).

Global cross-agent review of S01..S04: AC1 (route returns 200 + PDF body + Content-Disposition + warning logged + pdf_path unchanged) every clause has a corresponding test assertion; AC2 (regression test exists + EXPECTED_5XX entry removed) verified end-to-end; docs_pdf and docs_pdf_view guards are structurally symmetric (same comment, same logger, same warning format); render_pdf_chromium patch path matches the actual import; ProjectDoc constructor in the test sets all live NOT NULL columns. Scope check: `git diff --name-only origin/main` is limited to dashboard/routers/docs.py + tests/dashboard/test_docs_pdf_cache_failure.py + tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py + ai-dev/active/I-00109/**. Full unit + integration suites run.
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)
QV: Integration tests — runs the dashboard route-sweep and the new docs_pdf cache-failure regression test
